summary: Implemented the M5 Opportunity Detail and limited status-update slice. Added a server-rendered /opportunities/{id} page backed by lib/opportunities.ts, a plain serializable detail view model, restrained detail UI components, a local PATCH /api/opportunities/{id} status path for saved/discarded/build_next only, focused tests, and README documentation. No MVP Spec generation/copy, schema migration, package change, scan orchestration, scoring change, auth/session, deployment, or task non-goal was implemented.

tests_run.result: PASS: 14 focused opportunity tests passed across 3 suites, covering detail serialization, score/risk/monetization/detail rendering, status allow-list parsing, valid status update handling, extra request fields not reaching the update boundary, invalid/new/built status rejection, malformed JSON handling, and safe not-found status errors.
PASS: Next.js production build completed successfully. The local Windows SWC optional native package printed nonfatal 'not a valid Win32 application' warnings before Next continued and exited 0.
PASS: no whitespace errors. Git printed LF-to-CRLF working-copy warnings for README.md, lib/opportunities.ts, and tests/opportunities.test.ts.

decisions: Kept Opportunity detail reads and status writes centralized in lib/opportunities.ts so Prisma query/update logic remains outside UI components.

Extended the existing Opportunity card serializer into serializeOpportunityDetail() rather than creating a parallel model, preserving plain JSON-safe view models for server/client rendering and tests.
Used Next notFound() from the /opportunities/{id} server page when the helper returns no persisted Opportunity or receives an invalid/missing id.
Implemented PATCH /api/opportunities/{id} as the narrow local mutation boundary, accepting only saved, discarded, and build_next while rejecting new, built, and arbitrary statuses.
Returned sanitized API errors for invalid JSON, invalid statuses, missing ids, missing opportunities, and unexpected failures without echoing internal ids or exception details.
Built the status UI as a small client component that receives serializable props and calls the local PATCH route; it reflects the current status and disables the active action.
Displayed score breakdown/explanation only from persisted score JSON, tool concept from rawAnalysis.opportunityAnalysis.toolConcept when available, and kill criteria from persisted killCriteria JSON.
Documented the local single-admin assumption and explicit non-goals in README instead of implying public auth, multi-user workflow, market proof, SEO certainty, or MVP Spec generation.
Lead orchestrator added extra focused API tests after the initial implementation handoff so malformed JSON, status=new rejection, and extra request fields are covered before reviewer routing.
risks: The status update endpoint is intentionally unauthenticated for the local single-admin MVP; public deployment still requires a later auth/session/security slice.
No discard reason, audit trail, optimistic cross-tab sync, or multi-user workflow is implemented; status changes are a simple persisted field update.
Tool concept details depend on rawAnalysis shape from the existing opportunity-analysis agent output; older rows without that JSON render the persisted score/toolability text that is available.
The local build environment prints nonfatal Windows SWC optional-binary warnings during Next build, although the build exits 0.
requested_action: Review M5-OPPORTUNITY-DETAIL-STATUS-001 against architecture and mutation-boundary requirements, then route to domain_designer, security_reviewer, and qa_reviewer for independent acceptance.
